mnemonics.title: Induction Steps
mnemonics.content: B-A-I: Base case, Assume (inductive hypothesis), Illustrate (prove for k+1)

mnemonics.title: Remember WOP
mnemonics.content: WOP: Well-Ordering = Every positive set has a Smallest (minimum) element

acronyms.usage: The statement we assume true for arbitrary k in induction proof
Foundation principle stating every non-empty positive integer set has least element
Proof technique: Base case + Inductive step = True for all n

visualTips.visualization: Think of induction as falling dominoes: Base case knocks over first domino, each domino knocks the next. If first falls and each triggers the next, all fall.

visualTips.visualize: P(k) → P(k+1) shown as: [P(k) is TRUE] → [P(k+1) becomes TRUE], like a chain reaction.
Weak: ONE previous case → next. Strong: ALL previous cases → next. Think of weak as single domino, strong as row of dominoes supporting next.

analogies.analogy: Like climbing an infinite ladder: prove you can reach first rung (base case), and that each rung has a ladder to reach the next (inductive step). Then you can reach any rung.
Like finding the smallest coin in a pile - there must always be a smallest one if coins represent positive integers.
Like building a tower: to build floor k+1, you need all floors 1 to k already built. In weak induction, you only need the immediately previous floor.
